rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY B AGONOY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) A filed stamped copy of the petition and proposed order have not been provided despite the hearing being continued 4 times. 2) Notice of Hearing has not been filed. 3) The accounting exceeds the number of years allowed under Local Rule 14.35A. 4) Petitioner requests a determination of those entitled to distribution; however, such petitions are generally filed prior to filing a petition for final distribution. It is also noted that the petition does not identify the proposed heirs. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JOYCE S. JEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/21. (No earlier date available.) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be paid before an account can be approved. The FMV of assets on hand shown in the accounting differ from the amount shown on the Vanguard statement. The second supplement filed on 3/11/19 was apparently to correct a mischaracterization of a time entry, which should not be charged against the estate. Attorney costs should show what was filed or sent by courier if the dates do not correspond to a filing in the Court's records. For example, there was nothing filed by the conservator on 11/27/18 or 2/5/19. It is unclear why delivery to Dept. 202 on 12/21/18 was $35 when the courier filed on the same date and adding a stop is usually only $5. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner no later than 3/8/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/2021. 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. 3) Petitioner must file notice being given on the mandatory notice of hearing form. 4) A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. Unless otherwise permitted for good cause, if an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three (3) years and each three- year period must be filed separately. [LRSF 14.35]. 5) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 6) Please explain why $651,685.83 is currently held in the Bank of America account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. 7) Please file the Seller's closing statement for the sale of the real property. 8) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.36 G.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN IRENE SODERLUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/21. (No earlier date available.) An original bank statement must be filed to verify the cash on hand at the end of the accounting period, not preceding the ending date of the accounting period. The statement attached to the petition is for the month ending 8/31/20. The accounting period ends 9/30/20. A reconciliation must be shown if there is any difference between the accounting and the bank statement. A declaration signed by the attorney or the administrator, whoever is in possession of the original, must also be filed to confirm that that person will retain the original for the requisite time; the declaration must include the language in LRSF 2.11 M.4. If cash after receipt of distribution from the husband's estate is $293,327.95, the net amount shown in paragraph XXIV appears to be incorrect. The claim filed by James Moreno may not be for more than the actual funeral costs ($4,847.65) and could have been paid out of the estate pursuant to Probate Code Sections 11420 and 11421. No interest is payable to James Moreno; the statute cited on the creditor's claim would have applied to the mortuary, had the funeral expenses not been paid within 60 days. Attorney costs must be itemized to show when they were paid, to whom, and for what purpose. It appears that an amendment to the petition will need to be filed. If the amendment cannot be filed in time to give notice before the 3/17/21 hearing, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request more time. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner no later than 3/8/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS ELKING WESTERBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. Proof of service filed on 1/20/21 shows the wrong (different) address for Bethany Autumn Shade Swart. Either the third supplement set forth her address incorrectly or notice was not sent to the correct address. Nothing has been filed to support the petitioner's allegation that he was told by first cousins about the decedent's half-brother who reportedly predeceased without issue. (Children or grandchildren of a cousin are first cousins once, or twice, removed, as the case may be, not second cousins.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition: For Removal, Appointment, Or Confirmation Of Trustee; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Damages; To Compel Trust Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/21 due to opposing Counsel's unavailability and also because of lack of courtesy copies and proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.A. and B.] Courtesy copies of the petition and all related papers and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner no later than 2/25/21. (It does not appear that the correct form of Notice of Hearing was served if this is an 850 petition.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MATTIE B. LYONS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: Determination That The Mattie B. Lyons Revocable Trust Is Invalid And Imposition Of Constructive Trust-Undue Influence.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/2021. 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. 3) Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined before the underlining petition will be determined. The Petition on page 3 lines 12-15 alleges the principal place of administration is San Bernardino. Please provide the current trustee's address and legal authority for San Francisco being the proper venue. 4) Please give notice to Harold Lyons not just his attorney. (Probate Code 1202). 5) Please file a copy of the current deed for 234 Minerva Street San Francisco. 6) Please file a copy of the Notification by Trustee. 7) Please explain page 2 line 28 and page 3 line 1 allege the petitioners will benefit by a juridical determination that the purported trust amendments are invalid. There appear to be no trust amendments just a trust attached to the petition please explain. 8) Please file legal authority in support of the causes of action.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANN M. LEHMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/3/2021. 1) The petition on Attachment 2a alleges a duly authenticated copy will be filed in this estate prior to the hearing. This does not appear to have been filed. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) Please be aware the Declaration of Property form is no longer required.
